turns.name: JOHN G. ROBERTS, JR.
turns.text: Justice Alito has the opinion of the court in case 21-1576 Smith v. United States.

turns.name: SAMUEL A. ALITO, JR.
turns.text: Inthis case, the United States secured a conviction against petitioner Timothy Smith for theft of trade secretsfollowing a trial in the Northern District of Florida. On appeal, the Eleventh Circuit concluded that the Trialhad been held in the wrong venue, but they disagreed with Smith that the Constitution prevents the government from retrying him in the correct venue. Therefore, the court vacated the conviction with the understanding that the government could retry Smith. We granted certiorari to resolve only whether the constitution precludes retrial in the correct venue following a Trial in the wrong venue. For reasons explained in the opinion we agree with the Eleventh Circuit that the Constitution does not preclude retrial in this circumstance, we therefore affirm the opinion of the court is unanimous.
